short_description: Download & Save Peekvids Videos Easily for Offline Viewing (Browser Extension)
long_description: This extension is designed to help you download publicly accessible video clips from Peekvids, focusing on the distinctive /v/{slug}/ URL pattern used throughout the platform. It works by detecting media streams that become available during playback, particularly through the iframe-based player transition common on Peekvids clip pages.

faqs.question: Does the extension work on all Peekvids pages?
faqs.answer: It is designed for the standard /v/{slug}/ clip route where iframe-based playback is used. Other page types may not be supported.

faqs.question: Is this extension officially affiliated with Peekvids?
faqs.answer: No. This is an independent browser extension built to work with the public Peekvids platform. It is not endorsed by or affiliated with Peekvids.

faqs.question: Can I download videos in resolutions other than what is listed?
faqs.answer: The extension captures the streams made available during playback. Available quality options depend on what the Peekvids player exposes through the iframe handoff.

faqs.question: What happens to my downloaded files?
faqs.answer: Files are saved to your browser's default download location. You can move, rename, or organize them as you wish.

faqs.question: Will the extension work if Peekvids changes its player?
faqs.answer: The extension may need updates if Peekvids significantly alters its playback structure. Check the releases page for updates.

permissions.description: Peekvids organizes its content around short voyeur-style clips, each served through a compact /v// URL. The platform uses an iframe-based playback model where the video player lives inside an embedded layer, making direct media access less straightforward than on simpler sites. Most generic downloaders fail to recognize this handoff pattern, leaving you unable to save clips you have permission to keep.
Archiving clips you have permission to keep for personal reference
Only download content you own or have explicit permission to save

why: Peekvids organizes its content around short voyeur-style clips, each served through a compact /v/{slug}/ URL. The platform uses an iframe-based playback model where the video player lives inside an embedded layer, making direct media access less straightforward than on simpler sites. Most generic downloaders fail to recognize this handoff pattern, leaving you unable to save clips you have permission to keep.

This extension is built with the Peekvids structure in mind. It understands the /v/ route pattern, watches for the iframe handoff during playback, and attempts to capture the underlying m3u8 or mp4 stream when it becomes available. The approach is deliberate rather than aggressive, giving you a reliable way to download clips without disrupting your browsing experience.
how_it_works: Install the extension from the latest release.
Open Peekvids and go to a supported video page.
Start playback so the extension can detect the media.
Open the popup or use the on-page controls.
Choose the quality option you want.
Start the download and wait for the MP4 export to finish.
tutorial: Navigate to the Peekvids clip page you want to save. The URL should follow the /v/{slug}/ pattern.
Allow the page to load completely, including the embedded iframe player.
Click play on the video to start playback. This activates the stream detection process.
Look for the extension icon in your browser toolbar. A badge may appear when media is detected.
Click the extension icon to open the popup interface.
Review the detected media sources. The extension will list available stream options.
Select your preferred quality or stream variant.
Click the download button and wait for the MP4 file to be prepared and saved.

Evaluating the extension as a candidate for Peekvids-specific download needs
troubleshooting: No download button appears on the Peekvids page Ensure you are on a supported /v/{slug}/ page and that the video player has started playback. The extension needs to detect the iframe handoff before it can offer download options.

The extension detects the page but finds no media streams Try refreshing the page and starting playback again. Some clips may have stricter stream exposure timing, and the iframe handoff may require a fresh page load.
Downloads fail or produce partial files Check your internet connection and ensure the Peekvids page remains open during the download. Interrupted network requests can cause incomplete file transfers.
The popup shows an error about unsupported content Not all Peekvids pages use the same iframe handoff pattern. The extension works best on standard /v/ clip routes with embedded players.
The extension icon does not appear after installation Make sure you have installed the correct build for your browser and that the extension is enabled in your browser settings.

about: Peekvids is a video platform centered around short voyeur-style clips, each served through a compact /v/{slug}/ URL structure. The platform uses an iframe-based playback model that makes media detection less straightforward, which is why this extension focuses specifically on understanding and working with that handoff pattern.
